caption:
Stop fighting CSS specificity.

@layer lets you control which styles win — regardless of selector specificity. No more !important. No more BEM workarounds.

All browsers since 2022.

Which approach do you use? A or B?

#css #csslayers #webdev #coding #programming #developer #frontend #specificity #cleancode #codingtips

a.label: Specificity hacks

b.label: @layer

slides.title: B wins. You control the cascade.

verdicts.title: Hacks that break later
verdicts.text: Repeating selectors or !important creates an arms race. Every override needs MORE specificity. It never ends.

verdicts.title: Layers define priority
verdicts.text: The order of @layer declarations determines which layer wins. Lower specificity in a higher layer beats higher specificity in a lower layer.

slides.takeaway: @layer solves the problem !important was invented for.

slides.title: The real power: third-party CSS.
slides.sub: Put Tailwind, Bootstrap, or any library in a low-priority layer.

items.text: @layer a, b, c; — declare priority order
Last declared layer has highest priority

slides.teaser: Next: CSS :has() — the parent selector that killed JavaScript
